_comment: Wiring map for the required-status-check drift watcher (see .github/scripts/required_check_drift.py). 'required_context' is the EXACT status-check context string that must stay listed as a required check on each repo's default branch. A reusable workflow called via 'uses:' emits its check as '<caller-job-id> / <reusable-job-name>', which for lockfile-registry is 'lockfiles / No lockfile references a Replit-internal registry host' (NOT the workflow display name). 'repos' lists the six repos that actually commit lockfiles and had the requirement wired (Task #308). 'mechanism' records HOW each requirement was originally set (ruleset = the 'series-a-default-branch' branch ruleset; classic = branches/main/protection/required_status_checks) and is informational only — the checker passes a repo if the context is required via EITHER mechanism, so an intentional ruleset rename or a mechanism swap does not false-alarm. Re-add a repo here if it later starts committing a lockfile and gets wired.
required_context: lockfiles / No lockfile references a Replit-internal registry host
